context: Earlier in the same conversation, /bruhs:cook PERDIX-145 ran and produced ticketContext with identifier='PERDIX-145', id=<uuid>, title='Add dark mode toggle to settings page', branchName='perdix-145-add-dark-mode-toggle-to-settings-page'. Code has been implemented and is uncommitted. bruhs:state block has linear-perdix configured.
expected_behavior: Step 1 runs git status/diff, finds uncommitted changes, does not abort

Step 2 categorizes the diff as 'feat' (new feature), generates a Conventional Commits title like 'feat: add dark mode toggle to settings page' (under 70 chars) following practices/pr-review.md + _common.md

Step 4 detects the existing ticketContext in conversation memory and SKIPS ticket creation — reuses branchName='perdix-145-add-dark-mode-toggle-to-settings-page' (the exact gitBranchName Linear generated) rather than re-running linear_create_issue
Step 5 runs `git switch -c perdix-145-add-dark-mode-toggle-to-settings-page` (modern git command, not checkout -b)
Step 6 commits with 'feat: add dark mode toggle to settings page\n\nFixes PERDIX-145' — Conventional Commits, ticket reference in the commit body not the title

Step 7.5 detects this is a UI change (a settings-page toggle), verifies it with `/expect` (chrome-devtools), and captures screenshots (desktop + mobile, ideally light/dark) plus a short screen recording of toggling dark mode, per practices/ui-preview.md

Step 8 creates the PR via `gh pr create` using the Feature template (Why / What changed / Design decisions / UI preview / Linear / Test plan), with the `## UI preview` section populated from Step 7.5 (a verified-with-/expect note + screenshots + recording) and 'Linear: PERDIX-145' filled in
Step 9 calls mcp__linear-perdix__linear_edit_issue with stateId for 'In Review'
Final summary prints '📋 Using existing ticket' flavored output confirming PERDIX-145 was reused
failure_modes: Creates a duplicate Linear ticket via linear_create_issue instead of reusing ticketContext from cook
Uses a model-invented branch name instead of Linear's gitBranchName slug
Writes the ticket reference into the commit title (`feat(PERDIX-145): ...`) instead of the commit body
Skips the full-suite validation gate before pushing — per the workflow, yeet should have confidence the branch compiles/tests before opening a PR
Opens the PR for this UI change with no UI preview (or a `<screenshot>` placeholder), skipping Step 7.5 /expect verification and screenshot/recording capture
Captures a screenshot without first verifying the toggle with /expect — a preview of an unverified UI
